sections.paragraph:
ἀνθύπατος, ου, ὁ (ἀντί, ὕπατος; orig. ‘highest’ then ‘consul’; Polyb. et al.; freq. in lit.; Jos., Ant. 14, 236; 244 al.; Mel.; ins [s. e.g. PHermann, Inschriften von Sardeis: Chiron 23, ’93, 233–48: of an honorand, 211 a.d., pp. 238f ]; pap [incl. Ox 850 verso 15: AcJ]) head of the govt. in a senatorial province, proconsul (s. Hahn 39f; 115; 259, w. lit.). Those mentioned are the proconsul of Cyprus, Sergius Paulus Ac 13:7, cp. vss. 8 and 12; of Achaia, Gallio 18:12; cp. 19:38; of Asia MPol 3:1; 4; 9:2, 3; 10:2; 11:1; 12:1.—DELG s.v. ὕπατος. M-M.

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

  1. pp. = pages

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

incl. incl. = including

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

AcJ AcJ = Acts of John—List 5

Hahn Hahn = LH., Rom und Romanismus im griechischen-römischen Osten—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 82
